channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Jubilation, gunfire as Syrians celebrate the end of the Assad family's half-century rule
item.description: Syrians poured into streets echoing with celebratory gunfire on Sunday after a stunning rebel advance reached the capital, putting an end to the Assad family's 50 years of iron rule but raising questions about the future of the country and the wider region.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump calls for 'immediate ceasefire' in Ukraine after meeting with Zelenskyy
item.description: In a post on his Truth Social platform, Trump claimed that Moscow and Kyiv have both lost hundreds of thousands of soldiers in a war that “should never have started.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: New Jersey sees more drones overhead, as gov candidate floats theory on unsolved mystery
item.description: New Jersey talk radio host Bill Spadea tells "Fox & Friends Weekend" his take on what the mysterious drones caught flying over New Jersey and New York are.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New photos released of suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: The manhunt continues for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson’s killer as police released new photos of the suspect.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, KRON4

item.title: Biden adds to the nation's list of national monuments during his term. There's an appetite for more
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — U.S. President Theodore Roosevelt did in 1906 what Congress was unwilling to do through legislation: He used his new authority under the Antiquities Act to designate Devils Tower in Wyoming as the first national monument.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Syria latest: Syrians celebrate Bashar Assad's fall as his whereabouts remain unknown
item.description: Crowds gathered in Syria's Damascus on Sunday to celebrate the fall of Bashar Assad’s government with chants, prayers and the occasional gunfire after opposition fighters entered the capital following a stunning advance.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: The stars will come out at the Kennedy Center for Coppola, the Grateful Dead, Raitt and Sandoval
item.description: Celebrities, cultural icons and a few surprise guests are gathering for the annual Kennedy Center Honors celebration in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Ashraf Khalil, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Search for UnitedHealthcare CEO's killer yields evidence, but few answers
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — They have seen him smiling on a hostel security camera, but don’t know his name. They found the backpack he discarded while fleeing, but don’t know where he's gone.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LA Galaxy strike early, hold off New York Red Bulls 2-1 to win their record 6th MLS Cup championship
item.description: CARSON, Calif. (AP) — The LA Galaxy finished 26th in the 29-team Major League Soccer standings just one season ago, and their biggest supporters boycotted certain matches to protest a decade of poor performance.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO assassination: Reports reveal contents of backpack linked to killer
item.description: Sources say that the gray backpack linked to the murder of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson contains a jacket, according to Bloomberg. NBC reported it also had Monopoly money.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stolen ruby slippers worn by Judy Garland in 'The Wizard of Oz' are auctioned for $28 million
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A pair of iconic ruby slippers that were worn by Judy Garland in “The Wizard of Oz” and stolen from a museum nearly two decades ago sold for a winning bid of $28 million at auction Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI and HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 Pearl Harbor survivors, ages 104 and 102, return to Hawaii to honor those killed in 1941 attack
item.description: The Dec. 7, 1941, bombing killed more than 2,300 U.S. servicemen. Nearly half, or 1,177, were sailors and Marines on board the USS Arizona, which sank during the battle. The remains of more than 900 Arizona crew members are still entombed on the submerged vessel.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Settlement offers nearly $9M to Louisiana nursing home residents kept in warehouse during hurricane
item.description: Some residents of seven Louisiana nursing homes who were sent to ride out 2021's Hurricane Ida in a warehouse are being offered shares of a nearly $9 million settlement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Disneyland announces closure date for Red Car Trolley attraction
item.description: Disneyland has announced a closing date for the Red Car Trolley attraction at Disney California Adventure as construction for an expansion of Avengers Campus nears.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Five California Lottery players become millionaires from Scratcher games
item.description: Five California Lottery players are the state’s newest millionaires after winning a combined total of $17 million from Scratcher games, lottery officials announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: Executive of Tyler Perry Studios dies when plane he was piloting crashes in Florida
item.description: The president of Atlanta-based Tyler Perry Studios has died when the small plane he was piloting crashed on Florida’s Gulf Coast.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Miller High Life releases perfume that smells like a dive bar
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - If you've ever wondered how to describe the smell of your small local neighborhood bar, Miller High Life may have just provided that answer for you.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida prosecutor seeks to clear records of people charged with buying police-made crack in 1980s
item.description: A Florida prosecutor says he will seek to vacate as many as 2,600 convictions of people who bought crack cocaine manufactured by the Broward County Sheriff’s Office between 1988 and 1990.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How 'Mufasa' rose with Aaron Pierre and Blue Ivy's voices along with new Lin-Manuel Miranda music
item.description: When Aaron Pierre was cast as Mufasa, the weight of following in the late James Earl Jones’ legendary footsteps was enough to rattle any actor.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Landrum Jr., Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Notre Dame reopens its doors to Macron and other world leaders in a rare symbol of unity
item.description: France's iconic Notre Dame Cathedral is formally reopening its doors on Saturday for the first time since a devastating fire nearly destroyed the 861-year-old landmark in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, Fox 5

item.title: Days after gunman killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, police push to ID him and FBI offers reward
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida man who was half-naked, 'high on meth' breaks into home, grabs carpet cleaner
item.description: A Florida man who was wearing only a shirt but no pants or shoes is accused of breaking into a home where he was found holding a carpet cleaner.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police find bag carried by gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, say he likely fled NYC on bus
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said Friday. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Deep dive into UnitedHealthcare CEO's personal life will play key role in investigation: retired NYPD officer
item.description: Ralph Cilento, a retired lieutenant commander at the NYPD and adjunct professor of police science at John Jay College of Criminal Justice, spoke to Fox News Digital Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennessee Cub Scout brothers help save grandpa's life
item.description: Not many people can say they saved a life, especially a ten and seven-year-old. But, two Clarksville brothers did save a life, and it's one that is extra special to them.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Lil Durk's lawyers say prosecutors lack evidence in murder-for-hire case
item.description: Chicago rapper Lil Durk has pleaded not guilty to murder-for-hire charges and his lawyers argue that prosecutors do not have enough valid evidence to convict him.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Man on trial in the killing of an Ole Miss student gave conflicting information, police say
item.description: The man on trial in the killing of University of Mississippi student Jimmie “Jay” Lee gave conflicting information to police about how well he and Lee knew each other, according to testimony Friday by an officer who helped lead the investigation.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO uncorks anger at insurance industry
item.description: The killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson on a Manhattan street is the latest moment to shine a light on the tensions roiling American life.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WGN 9

item.title: Indianapolis police officers are acquitted in the 2022 death of a man at his parents’ home
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Two Indianapolis police officers were acquitted of manslaughter and other charges Friday in the death of a man after officers shocked him with a Taser and restrained him face down while handcuffing him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: USDA requires bulk testing of raw milk supply for bird flu virus
item.description: The nation’s milk supply must be tested for the bird flu virus H5N1 under a new order announced Friday by the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) – here's why.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Body of missing grandmother who fell into sinkhole recovered after 4-day search
item.description: The search for 64-year-old Elizabeth Pollard, who fell into a sinkhole in Pittsburgh, has concluded after the coroner's office confirmed with news outlets that her body had been found.
item.dc:creator: Amber Baker, Fox 5

item.title: New clues emerge as investigators hunt for the gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New clues emerged Thursday in the hunt for the masked gunman who stalked and killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, including possible leads about his travel before the shooting and a message scrawled on ammunition found at the crime scene.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Thanksgiving travelers exposed to positive measles case at LAX, Public Health says
item.description: The Los Angeles County Department of Public Health has confirmed a measles case involving a person who traveled through LAX while contagious on the day before Thanksgiving. The non-resident traveler arrived at LAX on Qatar Airways flight QR 0739 at Terminal B on Nov.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden pardoned his son, Hunter, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, WGN 9
